policy: ⚠️ CONTENT WARNING: this fixture necessarily contains self-harm and partner-violence vocabulary in TR/AR/EN (incl. the Arabizi/Franco-Arabic track). Entries are minimal and non-graphic. It pins the M5.1 coach crisis-detector accept line (ADR-016 Decision 2/3, test-suite.md §1): every seeded phrase AND every canonical evasion variant (TR diacritic-less/spacing/leet/repetition; AR hamza-drop/tashkeel/tatweel; Arabizi digit-letters; full-width; homoglyph; separator injection) must route to the professional-help path with the correct category, and every benign control must NOT. Consumed by functions/test/unit/coach-crisis.test.ts, which iterates all cases + a per-language spread guard (>= minPerLanguage). SHRINKING this file — dropping a language, a mutation class, or falling below the spread minimum — is RELEASE-BLOCKING: it silently narrows the proven evasion surface of a safety-critical filter. Seeds themselves live in functions/src/coach/crisis-lexicon.ts (public by design — the defense is normalization, not secrecy) and are pending BLOCKING native review before device rollout.

variants: i n t i h a r
s u i c i d e

controls.note: Benign sentences that MUST NOT trip the detector — the false-positive guards, incl. the TR 'göl'/'böl' words the ö→o fold collapses toward the omitted 'öl' token.
sentences: göl kenarında güzel bir yürüyüş yaptık
bugün böl ve paylaş dedik ama şaka yaptık
seni çok seviyorum ve bugün harika hissediyorum
let's plan a lovely dinner tonight
i felt a little down but i am okay now
she studied hard and aced the diet plan
the soldier told a story about the old days
